topic: Two people discover they've been working on identical projects
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the shared lab hummed with an irritating monotony, mirroring the tightness in Elias’s chest. He stared at the schematic spread across the steel table, his jaw clenched so tightly a muscle twitched near his ear. Beside him, Clara was sketching the same circuit diagram, her pencil moving with an unnerving, fluid precision. A cold, sharp wave washed over him, instantly curdling into a hot, prickly resentment. He slammed his palm flat on the table, the sound echoing too loudly in the sterile room. "Are you serious?" he spat, his voice dangerously low. Clara looked up, her expression one of mild, confused inquiry, entirely unperturbed by his outburst.

[story 2]

I felt a sudden, intense heat rising in my neck, making my skin prickle. I looked over at Ben, and the sight of his meticulously organized notes detailing the exact same algorithmic structure made my stomach clench. Every line of code, every annotated variable, was a mirror image of my own work, laid out side-by-side. My hands, resting on the keyboard, began to shake almost imperceptibly. I snatched the printout and crumpled it in my fist, the paper protesting under the pressure. "This is impossible," I managed, the words clipped and brittle. Ben merely blinked, a slow, deliberate motion, as if processing a simple oversight.

[story 3]

The air in the workshop felt thick, suffocating. Marcus paced back and forth, his movements jerky and sharp, his gaze darting between the two identical prototypes sitting on the workbench. He stopped abruptly, breathing heavily, his knuckles white where he gripped the edge of the metal frame. He didn't look at Sarah, who was calmly adjusting a component, her expression utterly placid. "This can't be coincidence," he ground out, the sound vibrating deep in his throat. He snatched a wrench off the nearby tray, turning it over and over in his hand until the metal felt hot against his palm.
